data.description:
# WS4 — Gate dispatch entry + source separation

## Why
The capability must be OFF by default and SOURCE-SEPARATED so it is inert when disabled, while preserving the existing public surface. This story adds the dispatch entry (parallel to plan-review) and isolates the code into its own optional module, retiring the throwaway single-pass route.

## What
- `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict(ctx, cfg, ...)` running `gates/code-review.yaml` in-memory (parallel to `produce_plan_review_verdict`), returning the typed `code_review_verdict` (`{verdict, blocking[], advisory[], coaching[], coverage}`) + a review sidecar.
- Source-separate `code_review.py` into an optional `rebar.llm.code_review` package; PRESERVE the public surface (the `review_code(...)` callable, the MCP `review_code` tool, the `rebar review-code` CLI keep names/signatures and `review_result` output) via re-export/shim.
- RETIRE the single-pass `evidence + aggregate_findings`-only route (unreachable); record the one-way-door removal in an ADR.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` — `produce_code_review_verdict`.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/__init__.py` — package + re-export shim.
- `src/rebar/mcp_server.py`, `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` — surface unchanged.
- `docs/adr/` — ADR for retiring the single-pass demo.
- OUT OF SCOPE: detectors (WS5), Gerrit (WS6).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `produce_code_review_verdict` runs the gate on the v3 engine and returns the typed `code_review_verdict` + emits a sidecar; OFF by default (config/env gated), inert when disabled.
- [ ] The public surface is preserved: `rebar.llm.review_code(...)`, MCP `review_code`, and `rebar review-code` keep names/signatures/`review_result`; a test exercises each.
- [ ] The single-pass GATE PATH is unreachable (no `evidence + aggregate_findings`-only route runs); an ADR records the removal.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the test suite pass.
data.title: WS4: gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict + source-separation (re-export shim, retire single-pass, ADR)
